content:
i forgot to reply to this, but in all seriousness and surprisingly -

Faros is actually from Lemuria, which is quite unusual in a sense, but in Lovecraftian works, Lemuria is a giga-continent essentially, so based on how she implicitly describes that her homeland was near to Oceania, and she isn't from the same tradition as Tulu and Miryam, she's probably from the other side of the continent. However, the entire continent experienced calamity at the same time nonetheless.

Australia (and Indonesia, Malaysia etc. I guess) did not get directly affected by the dissolution crisis at least at first, they sunk as well as part of the massive "geological changes" that also came as the dissolution crisis emerged.

In the lore, Faros actually got blamed by some for the sinking of Oceania, but that may or may not be true. In any case, before she was killed and became an awaker afterwards, she unleashed many apocalyptic storms in her grief-driven madness.

wild lore honestly
all of her actions were justified tbh (jk)
Damn I live in the ocean ig
So in the world of Morimens almost the entirety of Southeast Asia is gone
I mean I know we pride ourselves on our beaches but come on
we have become lemurians part of gtulu’s society
gtulu actually leads some angry SEAsians
GMiryam, in line with arc 1 miryam’s fondness for babies, actually sells balut
wonder if arc 2’s miryam is a GMiryam like the other crucial members of the seafarer like GTulu and GMurphy
GMiryam is the evil mother in law with an internalize misogyny mind
hameln getting ready to finish the job ||by putting a fragment to sleep||

Useless Karen origin lore:

Well, it says outright that she was a handmaiden in the dynasty of the Achaemenid Empire, but the lore mentions (and roasts her) for being "Erivanian"

I know an okay amount about the Achaemenid Empire, but I was struggling to figure out where she specifically was from, I kind of half-recognized the proverb

Anyways, she's got to be from modern-day Armenia, it just threw me off a lot since under the Achaemenids, the capital of the province in that era was Erebuni

After the Achaemenids, the nearby area evolved its name from Erebuni to Yerevan, so from there it's probably just chatgpt butchering the place name honestly. 

Although, it could possibly instead be a reference to Yervanduni, in any case, broadly similar location. Armenia was a bit infamous I suppose you could say for entering into questionable client kingdom agreements back-to-back for just over a thousand years straight
